GEO FUNDAMENTALS

What is Generative Engine Optimization?

Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the practice of optimizing content so that it is more likely to be cited, referenced, and surfaced by AI-powered generative search engines.

The New Gatekeepers

Millions of people now receive answers directly from large language models instead of clicking through traditional search results. These models synthesize information from across the web and present it as a single, authoritative response.

In this environment, being ranked #1 on Google is no longer enough. You must become one of the few sources that the model chooses to trust and cite.

Key Characteristics of GEO

  • Authority over ranking — Models prioritize trustworthy, expert sources
  • Originality matters — Unique insights and data outperform generic content
  • Structure enables citation — Clear, scannable content is easier for models to extract
  • E-E-A-T is amplified — Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness are critical
